title: rhel9: no pause image
body:
```console
# podman pod create --name foo
ERRO[0000] Error freeing pod lock after failed creation: no such file or directory
Error: error adding Infra Container: error pulling infra-container image: initializing source docker://registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/pause:latest: reading manifest latest in registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/pause: name unknown: Repo not found
```
Presumably because:
```console
# grep -R ubi9 /usr/share/containers/
/usr/share/containers/containers.conf:infra_image = "registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/pause"
```
podman-3.3.0-0.18.module+el9beta+12201+6a03aeff.x86_64

PS may I suggest that the "Error freeing pod lock" message be somehow shutupped in a situation like this?

comments.body: @jnovy We can just use the ubi8/pause image.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Appears to be fixed in 12467 (`podman-3.3.1-6.module+el9.0.0+12467+378c8264.x86_64`), built on 2021-09-01 for the latest:9.0.0 stream. I have not checked any other module builds, so I don't feel comfortable closing until I can know it is fixed in all possible rhel9 builds.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@edsantiago feeling any more comfortable to close?
I no longer know how to look at RHEL module-test logs. I see red (failed) on all recent builds, but don't know how to see what failed. @jnovy can you close this issue if it's fixed?

@edsantiago @rhatdan the ubi9 image exists but it hasn't been pushed to the registry yet as we are still way early in the RHEL9 GA cycle. That means pause can't be rebuilt against it just yet. I will close it as soon as I note ubi9 is pushed into the stage registry.
Hopefully with the work that @vrothberg is doing to make us stop using the Pause image, we can close this since it is not necessary in RHEL9. Do you agree @vrothberg ?
I agree.  We don't need to pull a pause image anymore but build it locally now.
